pages.title: CMMI Appraisal System

fields.name: TimeZone
options: (UTC+02:00) Helsinki, Kyiv, Riga, Sofia, Tallinn, Vilnius
(UTC+01:00) Amsterdam, Berlin, Bern, Rome, Stockholm, Vienna
(UTC-04:00) Georgetown, La Paz, Manaus, San Juan

fields.name: Objectives
fields.label: * Business and Appraisal Objectives
fields.value:
[BO1] Reduce residual defect density by 3%
[BO2] Improve productivity by 3%
[A1] In support of the business objectives, understand the current strengths and weaknesses of the CMMI Maturity Level 5 practices within the organization [BO1, BO2].

allLabels:
* Time Zone
      
        Time zone of the primary location where the appraisal is being conducted.
* Is any part of the appraisal being performed virtually?
* Select all phases and/or activities where you will conduct virtual activities:
2.3.3 Determine Practice Area and Maturity Level of Capability Level Profile

(C-19) Manufacturing - Manufacture of coke and refined petroleum products
(C-20) Manufacturing - Manufacture of chemicals and chemical products

(E-37) Water supply; sewerage, waste management and remediation activities - Sewerage
(E-38) Water supply; sewerage, waste management and remediation activities - Waste collection, treatment and disposal activities; materials recovery
(E-39) Water supply; sewerage, waste management and remediation activities - Remediation activities and other waste management services
(G-45) Wholesale and retail trade; repair of motor vehicles and motorcycles - Wholesale and retail trade and repair of motor vehicles and motorcycles
(G-46) Wholesale and retail trade; repair of motor vehicles and motorcycles - Wholesale trade, except of motor vehicles and motorcycles
(G-47) Wholesale and retail trade; repair of motor vehicles and motorcycles - Retail trade, except of motor vehicles and motorcycles
(H-49) Transportation and storage - Land transport and transport via pipelines
(H-52) Transportation and storage - Warehousing and support activities for transportation
(H-53) Transportation and storage - Postal and courier activities
(I-55) Accommodation and food service activities - Accommodation
(I-56) Accommodation and food service activities - Food and beverage service activities

(J-63) Information and communication - Information service activities
(K-64) Financial and insurance activities - Financial service activities, except insurance and pension funding
(K-65) Financial and insurance activities - Insurance, reinsurance and pension funding, except compulsory social security
(K-66) Financial and insurance activities - Activities auxiliary to financial service and insurance activities
(M-69) Professional, scientific and technical activities - Legal and accounting activities
(M-70) Professional, scientific and technical activities - Activities of head offices; management consultancy activities
(M-71) Professional, scientific and technical activities - Architectural and engineering activities; technical testing and analysis

(M-73) Professional, scientific and technical activities - Advertising and market research
(M-74) Professional, scientific and technical activities - Other professional, scientific and technical activities
(M-75) Professional, scientific and technical activities - Veterinary activities
(N-77) Administrative and support service activities - Rental and leasing activities
(N-78) Administrative and support service activities - Employment activities

(Q-88) Human health and social work activities - Social work activities without accommodation
(R-90) Arts, entertainment and recreation - Creative, arts and entertainment activities

fields.name: ParticipantGroup

* Participant Group
    Describe the group of individuals affected.

fields.name: ParticipantIndividuals
fields.label:
* Participant Individuals
    
      Name and list the number of individuals in the participant group, using the format Name (#).
      For example: Interviewees (4), Manager Interviewees (2). Lists with more than one type of individual
      should be separated by a comma (,).

      List task/activities/events from the appraisal schedule.

pages.title: CMMI Appraisal System

fields.name: LogisticalRequirement
options: Description of the location(s) within the OU where appraisal activities will take place
Accommodations and travel support for ATMs, as needed
Meals, snacks, beverages, etc. for the appraisal team and participants
Equipment and material requirements for ATMs.

fields.name: LogisticalRequirementEquipment
options: Access to the storage of OE on the OU network
Appropriate computer equipment for the appraisal team to use throughout the appraisal
Security and access needs (e.g., badges, escorts, etc.)
A room or rooms that can be used for performing interviews and delivering presentations
Appropriate telecommunications equipment and rooms to accommodate interviews conducted using virtual techniques
A facility with a projector available for performing the opening presentation findings briefings
Access to a printer that can be used to print potentially appraisal-confidential information
Office supplies (e.g., self-sticking flip chart paper, notepads or other paper, white board pens, magic markers, writing pens)
Projector(s) for use throughout the appraisal, as needed
A mechanism for use by the appraisal team to securely dispose of notes and intermediate work products to protect confidentiality and non-attribution.

fields.name: OtherRequirement
fields.label:
* Other logistical requirement
    Name the logistical requirement if "other" was selected from the dropdown

allLabels:
* Logistical requirement Equipment
    
      Specify the required equipment if "Equipment and material requirements for ATMs." was selected
      from the dropdown

* Other logistical requirement
    Name the logistical requirement if "other" was selected from the dropdown

pages.title: CMMI Appraisal System

fields.name: Type
options: Team member authors one or more processes
Team member is a member of the organization's process group
Team member is a process owner
Team member has a supervisory authority over one or more parts of the OU
Team member has served on previous appraisals in the same OU
Team member has been interviewed or provided evidence
Team member is involved in process or product quality audits
Team member provided coaching, consulting, or labor for the OU in support of this appraisal
Team member is internal to the organization being appraised
Translator works for a consulting company that has supported the OU with process development or implementation
Appraisal Team Leader is internal to the OU (only applies to Evaluation appraisals)
